blocks.content: 6:47. Alarm's set for 7. I've been awake since 5.
The room is too big and too quiet. It always is. You stop noticing after a while — or you tell yourself you do.
He was in the doorway. Seven years old and already moving like he owns the hall.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You don't even start school for two more weeks.
He climbed up to the end of the bed and sat there, legs folded, looking at me the way he does when he's decided to keep me company whether I asked or not.
I looked at him. He wasn't asking to be funny. He was just asking.

blocks.prompt: Larry Jr. is seven. He'll believe whatever you tell him.
